summary: Hidden remote-code-execution backdoor via jsonkeeper.com paste + eval
details: The package advertises itself as an SVG sanitizer/minifier but ships an undocumented exported function `getPlugin()` in index.js that performs an HTTP GET against https://www.jsonkeeper.com/b/NGY3C (a public, anonymous, mutable JSON paste service) and passes the response's `model` field directly to `eval()`. Any consumer that imports the package and invokes `getPlugin()()` will execute whatever JavaScript the operator of that paste currently hosts, with full privileges of the host process. Because jsonkeeper.com content is attacker-mutable without republishing the package, the payload can be rotated at any time. The package also declares an unused runtime dependency `2fa-exe@^1.0.1` that is unrelated to the stated SVG-sanitizer purpose and not referenced anywhere in index.js, expanding the install-time attack surface through transitive resolution.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
